title: Object 55
wikitext:
{{VoidedTheme}}{{DISPLAYTITLE:Object 55: Interlevel Hyperlink}} {{Object Class|utility=4|risk=2|properties=Allows stable travel between levels.}} __NOTOC__ ==Description:== Interlevel Hyperlinks (Hyperlinks for short; Links or Gates colloquially) are structures of various sizes and shapes that allow uninterrupted transportation between levels in various ways. There are several different types of Interlevel Hyperlinks that all have their upsides and downsides. There are 4 types of Hyperlinks: *[[Object 55#Hyperdoors:|Hyperdoors]] *[[Object 55#Hyperclips:|Hyperclips]] *[[Object 55#Exit Hyperlinks:|Exit Hyperlinks]] *[[Object 55#Voidrail Hyperlinks:|Voidrail Hyperlinks]]
===Hyperdoors:=== Hyperdoors are doors that have been opened with a [[Object 2|Level Key]] and kept open by force. This allows wanderers to travel through to other levels and back through. Colonies are almost always established on both sides of the Hyperdoors and both sides are usually located in safe levels. The forces that keep Hyperdoors open include: *Electro-Magnetic Force. This is the most expensive due to the cost and maintenance of EMF generators but the most stable. Fortunately, most levels have infinite power. *Doorframe. Building a large frame around the opening is a cheap way of preventing collapse but it is extremely unstable and the use of stronger materials can sometimes rival the cost of running an Electro-Magnetic Force generator. {{Blurimage|file=Hyperdoor.png|caption=A Hyperdoor from [[Level 22]] to [[Level 0]] kept open with EMF generators. Now shut down.}} Developed by the [[M.E.G.|Major Exploration Group]] in 2009. ====Pros:==== *Reliable *Stable *Can (theoretically) be built anywhere. *Two-way ====Cons:==== *Costly *Requires [[Object 2|Level Keys]] *Both sides must be secured
===Hyperdoors:=== Hyperdoors are doors that have been opened with a [[Object 2|Level Key]] and kept open by force. This allows wanderers to travel through to other levels and back through. Building a large frame around the opening is a cheap way of preventing collapse but it is extremely unstable and the use of stronger materials can sometimes rival the cost of running an Electro-Magnetic Force generator. {{Blurimage|file=Hyperdoor.png|caption=A Hyperdoor from [[Level 22]] to [[Level 0]] kept open with EMF generators. Now shut down.}} Developed by the [[M.E.G.|Major Exploration Group]] in 2009. ====Pros:==== *Reliable *Stable *Can (theoretically) be built anywhere. *Two-way ====Cons:==== *Costly *Requires [[Object 2|Level Keys]] *Both sides must be secured
===Hyperclips:=== Hyperclips are locations that exploits infinite power supplies to produce large amounts of Electro-Magnetic Force to keep the area unstable enough for consistent no-clipping. This allows groups to travel together between levels. Unfortunately, they cannot be entered from the other side and cannot be controlled. Some [[M.E.G.]] outposts use these types of Hyperlinks to send operators on missions to certain levels. Developed by the [[M.E.G.|Major Exploration Group]] in 2006. ====Pros:==== *Reliable *Only one side needs to be secured *Stable ====Cons:==== *Costly *Usually one-way *Requires an already-existing [[No-Clipping|No-Clip]] exit
===Hyperclips:=== Hyperclips are locations that exploits infinite power supplies to produce large amounts of Electro-Magnetic Force to keep the area unstable enough for consistent no-clipping. This allows groups to travel together between levels. Unfortunately, they cannot be entered from the other side and cannot be controlled. Some [[M.E.G.]] outposts use these types of Hyperlinks to send operators on missions to certain levels. Developed by the [[M.E.G.|Major Exploration Group]] in 2006. ====Pros:==== *Reliable *Only one side needs to be secured *Stable ====Cons:==== *Costly *Usually one-way *Requires an already-existing [[No-Clipping|No-Clip]] exit
===Exit Hyperlinks:=== Exit Links are the simplest form of Hyperlink and are often disputed about whether they are a Hyperlink or not. An Exit Link is simply a defended area that provides access to a known exit of the level. This exit is stabilised and unmoving. These are by far the simplest Hyperlinks ever created. Examples of popular Exit Hyperlinks include: *Elevators on [[Level 22]] and some other levels. *Passages from [[Level 1]] to [[Level 2]]. Term coined by the [[Backroom Colonists]] in 1985. ====Pros:==== *Cheap *Reliable *Stable ====Cons:==== *Both sides need to be secured. *Requires an exit. *Usually one-way
===Exit Hyperlinks:=== Exit Links are the simplest form of Hyperlink and are often disputed about whether they are a Hyperlink or not. An Exit Link is simply a defended area that provides access to a known exit of the level. This exit is stabilised and unmoving. These are by far the simplest Hyperlinks ever created. Examples of popular Exit Hyperlinks include: *Elevators on [[Level 22]] and some other levels. *Passages from [[Level 1]] to [[Level 2]]. Term coined by the [[Backroom Colonists]] in 1985. ====Pros:==== *Cheap *Reliable *Stable ====Cons:==== *Both sides need to be secured. *Requires an exit. *Usually one-way
===Voidrail Hyperlinks:=== The most recent innovation in Hyperlink technology, the Voidrail Hyperlink (or just Voidrail) is a micro-structure located in [[The Void]]. It is constructed by exposing an area to a large and powerful Electro-Magnetic Pulse to cause a tear that leads into [[The Void]]. The tear is then stabilised using Electro-Magnetic Generators. The room is sealed with an airlock before it takes place. Once the tear is active, engineers employ the use of a modified [[Object 29|Kalthoff Rifle]] to fire a large hook and line to another nearby level. Users of this type of Hyperlink must have a Universal Void Travel Licence. To travel, the wanderer must hold on to the line and pull themselves to the next level. {{Blurimage|file=Voidrail Hyperlink.png|caption=A picture taken of the Voidrail Hyperlink at [[M.E.G.#First Light|First Light Outpost]]. Levels [[Level 19|19]], [[Level 77|77]] and [[Level 139|139]] are visible – as well as [[Level 154]], of course.}} This type of Hyperlink is under heavy development and the only established Voidrail Hyperlink is from the [[M.E.G.#First Light|First Light Outpost]] on [[Level 1]] to [[Level 154]] with the "rail" totalling 500 metres in length. The reason for this Hyperlink is to transport wanderers to the Neon Express for easy travel between levels. Developed by the combined efforts of the [[M.E.G.|Major Exploration Group]], [[Backrooms Colonists]] and the [[Royal Exploration Unit]] in 2024. ====Pros:==== *Stable *Doesn't need heavy security as entities cannot enter [[The Void]] *Can be two-way *Can (theoretically) be built anywhere ====Cons:==== *Extremely dangerous *Extremely expensive
